data.title: U.S. Special Envoy Anticipates Talks with Iran This Week
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump's special envoy, Avi Berkowitz, announced plans to engage in discussions with Iran this week. According to PANews, Berkowitz expects a response soon from Iran regarding a 15-point ceasefire proposal put forth by the United States.

An Iranian armed forces spokesperson stated that Iran is formulating conditions for ending the conflict. The spokesperson warned the United States and Israel of Iran's formidable military strength and its ability to influence the war's outcome, suggesting that both nations will be 'forced' to acknowledge this reality. The spokesperson emphasized that the U.S. and Israel are well aware of Iran's military capabilities and the resilience of its people, advising them to face the facts and return to rationality.

data.title: Ripple CEO Anticipates Record First Quarter Amid Acquisitions
data.description:
Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse has announced that the company expects to achieve a record-breaking first quarter, driven by mergers and acquisitions. According to ChainCatcher, Ripple has invested approximately $4 billion into the crypto ecosystem, including the acquisition of Hidden Road for $1.25 billion and GTreasury for around $1 billion.

Garlinghouse also highlighted that the progress of U.S. digital asset legislation is slower than anticipated. He mentioned that the CLARITY Act, initially expected to be implemented in April, might be delayed until May. He urged that regulatory measures should not become overly politicized, as this could hinder the industry's development.

data.title: Circle Mints 500 Million USDC on Solana, Total Issuance Reaches $24.4 Billion
data.description: Circle has minted an additional 500 million USDC on the Solana blockchain, according to PANews. Over the past 30 days, the total issuance of USDC has reached $24.4 billion.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Highlights Bitcoin's Influence at Miami Summit
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump emphasized the significant impact of Bitcoin during the Future Investment Initiative Summit held in Miami. According to Odaily, he stated that the United States should maintain a leading position in the field of virtual assets. Trump noted the growing number of users interested in using virtual assets, particularly Bitcoin, for payments, underscoring the need for the U.S. to be at the forefront of these developments.

data.title: The Governor of California signed an executive order prohibiting public officials from using insider information to participate in prediction markets.
data.description: On March 27, California Governor Gavin Newsom signed Executive Order N-4-26, explicitly prohibiting public officials appointed by the governor from profiting from non-public information obtained in the course of their duties in prediction markets, and prohibiting them from assisting their spouses, children, and other related persons in such activities. The order points out that recent cases of insider trading in prediction markets involving geopolitical events have raised regulatory concerns. The California government stated that this move aims to strengthen ethical constraints, maintain public trust, and ensure that public officials focus on the public interest.

data.title: Trump: Bitcoin holds an important position, and the US needs to maintain its lead.
data.description: Speaking at the Future Investment Initiative Summit in Miami, Trump stated that Bitcoin has significant influence and that the United States should maintain a leading position in this field. He pointed out that more and more users want to use virtual assets, especially Bitcoin, for payments, and the US needs to be at the forefront of this development trend.

data.title: Ripple CEO: The Clarity Act may be delayed until May; approximately $4 billion has already been invested in the crypto ecosystem.
data.description: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse stated that the company expects a record first quarter driven by acquisitions, and noted that it has already invested approximately $4 billion in the crypto ecosystem by 2025, including the $1.25 billion acquisition of Hidden Road and the approximately $1 billion acquisition of GTreasury. He also pointed out that progress on US digital asset legislation is slower than expected, with the CLARITY Act potentially delayed from its original April date to May, and called for avoiding over-politicization of regulation that could negatively impact industry development. (The Block)

data.title: Bank of America Settles Epstein-Related Lawsuit for $72.5 Million
data.description: Bank of America has agreed to a $72.5 million settlement in a lawsuit accusing the bank of maintaining connections with individuals associated with Jeffrey Epstein and failing to report suspicious transactions. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the lawsuit alleged the bank did not adequately monitor and report activities that could have indicated illicit financial dealings. This settlement is part of ongoing legal actions related to Epstein's financial network, which has implicated several major financial institutions. The bank has not admitted any wrongdoing as part of the settlement agreement.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 66,000 USDT with a 4.06% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 27, 2026, 22:26 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 66,000 USDT and is now trading at 65,963.976563 USDT, with a narrowed 4.06% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Prediction Markets Process Over $154 Billion in Volume, Resembling Retail Trading Venues
data.description: Prediction markets have seen significant activity, processing more than $154 billion in total volume. According to NS3.AI, platforms like Polymarket often experience daily trading volumes exceeding $300 million. The analysis suggests that the characteristics of these markets, such as small trade sizes, frequent user activity, and active capital rotation, make them resemble retail trading venues rather than traditional betting markets.

data.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid Middle East Conflict Concerns
data.description: Oil prices have increased as traders anticipate ongoing disruptions to crude supply due to the Middle East conflict. According to RTHK, Brent crude in London closed at $112.57 per barrel, marking a rise of $4.56 or 4.2%. Meanwhile, New York crude settled at $99.64 per barrel, up by $5.16, representing an increase of nearly 5.5%. Over the week, Brent and U.S. crude futures have gained approximately 0.3% and over 1%, respectively.

data.title: European Stock Markets Close Lower with German DAX Falling Over 1%
data.description:
European stock markets ended the trading session with declines, with Germany's DAX index experiencing a significant drop. According to RTHK, the German DAX index closed at 22,300 points, down 312 points, marking a decrease of nearly 1.4%. France's CAC index ended at 7,701 points, falling by 67 points, or approximately 0.9%. Meanwhile, the UK's FTSE 100 index closed at 9,967 points, a slight decrease of 4 points, or about 0.1%.

Over the week, the German stock market fell by around 0.4%, marking its fourth consecutive week of decline. In contrast, both the French and UK stock markets saw gains of approximately 0.5%, breaking a three-week losing streak.

data.title: SEC Urged to Pursue Full ATS Registration for DeFi Platforms
data.description:
Plume General Counsel B. Salman Banaei has called on the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to implement full Alternative Trading System (ATS) registration for platforms utilizing decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols. According to NS3.AI, Banaei made this appeal during his testimony before the House on March 25. He emphasized the need for comprehensive regulation rather than relying primarily on a limited innovation exemption for tokenized trading.

The SEC is currently exploring a time-limited pathway for tokenized National Market System (NMS) equities to be traded on-chain. The agency is also in the process of gathering public feedback to develop a more extensive framework for the long-term regulation of these digital assets.

data.title: Trump Advocates for Low Interest Rates and Inflation-Free Economy
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed his desire for the United States to maintain low interest rates and achieve an economic environment free of inflation. According to ChainCatcher, Trump emphasized the importance of these economic conditions in fostering growth and stability. His comments reflect ongoing discussions about monetary policy and its impact on the nation's economy.

data.title: Trump's Signature to Appear on US Dollar Bills Starting June
data.description: The US Treasury Department has announced that U.S. President Donald Trump's signature will be featured on US dollar bills, beginning with the $100 notes in June. According to NS3.AI, this decision marks the first time a sitting president's signature will appear on US banknotes. Previously issued notes will continue to be legal tender indefinitely.

data.title: Hyperliquid's HYPE Experiences Price Decline Amid Bearish Market Structure
data.description: Hyperliquid's HYPE token saw a decrease in value, trading at $38.27, marking a 2.31% drop for the day. According to NS3.AI, the token has completed a double top pattern and faces a liquidation cluster at $35.03. The cumulative long liquidation leverage at this level amounts to $27.36 million. The article identifies $42.67 as the critical level that would invalidate the current bearish structure.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Faces Backlash Over Mandate Allegations
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation recently released a 38-page mandate, sparking controversy over claims that employees were required to pledge allegiance to its CROPS principles or face expulsion. According to NS3.AI, it is still uncertain if the Ethereum Foundation officially mandated such a pledge. The debate has since expanded to include discussions about the tone of the mandate and its Milady-inspired design.

data.title: Ukraine and Russia Show Signs of Involvement in Middle East Conflict
data.description: Ukraine and Russia are exhibiting signs of involvement in the Middle East conflict. According to Ming Pao, Ukraine has been exporting anti-drone technology to Middle Eastern countries, and President Volodymyr Zelensky announced on Friday, the 27th, that Ukraine has signed a defense cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. Meanwhile, European nations are concerned about Russia's potential plans to supply advanced drones to Iran.

data.title: U.S. Government Confirms Use of Autonomous Unmanned Vessels in Iran Conflict
data.description: The U.S. government has confirmed the deployment of autonomous unmanned vessels in the ongoing conflict with Iran, marking the first time such technology has been used in a battlefield setting. According to Ming Pao, these vessels are currently being utilized for patrol operations and have not yet been involved in any offensive actions. This development highlights the increasing role of advanced technology in modern warfare, as nations seek to enhance their strategic capabilities through automation and innovation.

data.title: Middle East Tensions Impact U.S. and Hong Kong Markets
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump expressed uncertainty about reaching an agreement with Iran, leading to a significant drop in U.S. stock markets. According to Ming Pao, subsequent negotiations between the U.S. and Iran showed progress, with Iran confirming receipt of a 15-point ceasefire proposal from the U.S. through a third party. Trump decided to postpone a major bombing campaign on energy infrastructure by ten days, which helped the Hang Seng Index recover from its morning low of 24,712 points, rising by as much as 239 points to reach 25,095 points. However, it failed to challenge the 250-day moving average, currently around 25,119 points. The Hong Kong stock market has experienced three 'Black Mondays' in the past four weeks, affecting investor sentiment over the weekend, causing the index to lose the 25,000-point mark.

Some stocks broke the recent trend of declining after earnings announcements. CSPC Pharmaceutical Group (1093) and Innovent Biologics (1801) rose by 13.8% and 7.68%, respectively, becoming the top gainers among blue-chip stocks. Ping An Insurance (2318) also saw a rise of 1.98%.
